title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
hints: Use three pointers to reverse the direction of each node's next pointer.
Iterate through the list, reversing the next pointer of each node to point to the previous node.

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Given head, the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it. There is a cycle in a linked list if there is some node in the list that can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer.
hints: Use two pointers, one moving one step at a time and the other moving two steps at a time.
If there is a cycle, the fast pointer will eventually meet the slow pointer.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2. Merge the two lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hints: Use a dummy head to simplify edge cases.
Compare nodes from both lists and attach the smaller one to the result.

title: Middle of the Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the middle node of the linked list. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node.
hints: Use the fast and slow pointer technique.
Move fast pointer two steps and slow pointer one step. When fast reaches the end, slow is at the middle.

title: Delete Node in a Linked List
description: There is a singly-linked list head and we want to delete a node node in it. You are given the node to be deleted node. You will be given no access to the head of the list, and you will only have access to the node to be deleted. The value of the head is guaranteed not to be the last node.
hints: Copy the value of the next node into the current node.
Then delete the next node.

title: Remove Linked List Elements
description: Given the head of a linked list and an integer val, remove all the nodes of the linked list that has Node.val == val, and return the new head.
hints: Use a dummy head to handle edge cases where the head itself needs to be removed.
Traverse the list and skip nodes with the target value.

title: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
description: Given the heads of two singly linked-lists headA and headB, return the node at which the two lists intersect. If the two linked lists have no intersection at all, return null.
hints: Two pointers can be used to traverse both lists.
When a pointer reaches the end, redirect it to the head of the other list. They will meet at the intersection.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List
description: Given the head of a sorted linked list, delete all duplicates such that each element appears only once. Return the linked list sorted as well.
hints: Traverse the list and compare each node with the next node.
If they have the same value, skip the next node.

title: Add Two Numbers
description: You are given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers. The digits are stored in reverse order, and each of their nodes contains a single digit. Add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list.
hints: Traverse both lists simultaneously, adding corresponding digits.
Keep track of the carry and create new nodes for the result.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def addTwoNumbers(self, l1: Optional[ListNode], l2: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        curr = dummy
        carry = 0
        while l1 or l2 or carry:
            val = carry
            if l1:
                val += l1.val
                l1 = l1.next
            if l2:
                val += l2.val
                l2 = l2.next
            carry = val // 10
            curr.next = ListNode(val % 10)
            curr = curr.next
        return dummy.next

title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head.
hints: Use a dummy head to handle edge cases.
Use two pointers with a gap of n nodes. When the fast pointer reaches the end, the slow pointer is at the node to remove.

title: Reorder List
description: You are given the head of a singly linked-list. The list can be represented as: L0 → L1 → … → Ln-1 → Ln. Reorder the list to be on the following form: L0 → Ln → L1 → Ln-1 → L2 → Ln-2 → …. You must not modify the values in the nodes' lists. Only nodes themselves may be changed.
hints: Find the middle of the list using fast-slow pointers.
Reverse the second half of the list.
Merge the two halves alternately.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def reorderList(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> None:
        if not head or not head.next:
            return
        slow, fast = head, head
        while fast.next and fast.next.next:
            slow = slow.next
            fast = fast.next.next
        prev, curr = None, slow.next
        slow.next = None
        while curr:
            nxt = curr.next
            curr.next = prev
            prev = curr
            curr = nxt
        first, second = head, prev
        while second:
            tmp1, tmp2 = first.next, second.next
            first.next = second
            second.next = tmp1
            first = tmp1
            second = tmp2

title: Odd Even Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, group all the nodes with odd indices together followed by the nodes with even indices, and return the reordered list. The first node is considered odd, and the second node is even, and so on.
hints: Use two pointers: one for odd-indexed nodes and one for even-indexed nodes.
Connect the odd list to the even list at the end.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def oddEvenList(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        if not head:
            return None
        odd = head
        even = head.next
        even_head = even
        while even and even.next:
            odd.next = even.next
            odd = odd.next
            even.next = odd.next
            even = even.next
        odd.next = even_head
        return head
